description: n8n workflow automation platform - configuration, deployment, and production gotchas for DMI instance (v2.8+)

execution_pruning.note: Successful executions are NOT saved. Only errors remain for debugging.

critical_gotchas_v28.description: CRITICAL: Hard-won lessons from production debugging. Read before working with n8n.

expression_evaluation.problem: {{ $json.xxx }} in HTTP Request node with contentType 'raw' is NOT evaluated in n8n v2.8
expression_evaluation.symptoms: Literal string '{{ $json.content }}' gets written/sent instead of actual data
solutions: BEST: Build complete body in Code node, output as single field, reference with ={{ $json.bodyField }}
expression_evaluation.note: In contentType 'json' (jsonBody field), {{ expressions }} work normally

if_node_operator.problem: IF node v2 operator 'boolean:equal' no longer recognized in v2.8
if_node_operator.note: Loose validation allows string 'true' to match boolean true

json_reference_after_http.problem: $json always refers to IMMEDIATE upstream node output. After HTTP Request, $json is the HTTP response, NOT your data.

code_node_console_log.problem: Code node v2 runs in separate JS Task Runner process. console.log is NOT visible in docker logs.
code_node_console_log.workaround: Query execution_data table in SQLite database to see node outputs

import_creates_duplicates.problem: n8n import:workflow ALWAYS creates a NEW workflow with a new ID. There is no update-in-place via CLI.
import_creates_duplicates.fix: For updates: delete old workflow from DB (webhook_entity, workflow_published_version, workflow_entity), then import:workflow fresh, then activate via update:workflow --active=true, then restart n8n.

execute_cli_port_conflict.problem: n8n execute CLI cannot run while n8n server is running
execute_cli_port_conflict.error_message: Task Broker's port 5679 is already in use
execute_cli_port_conflict.workaround: Use Manual Trigger in n8n UI or add temporary webhook trigger for testing

sqlite_wal_docker_cp.problem: docker cp of SQLite database may miss WAL file, causing database corruption
sqlite_wal_docker_cp.error_message: SQLITE_CORRUPT: database disk image is malformed
sqlite_wal_docker_cp.fix: Use dump/restore: sqlite3 old.db .dump | sqlite3 new.db
sqlite_wal_docker_cp.prevention: Always use docker exec with node scripts to query/update from INSIDE the container

docker_cross_network_communication.problem: Containers on different Docker networks (e.g. n8n and Uptime Kuma) cannot reach each other via localhost or container name
docker_cross_network_communication.fix: Use Docker bridge gateway IP 172.17.0.1 instead of localhost. This routes through the host network.
docker_cross_network_communication.example: Uptime Kuma webhook URL: http://172.17.0.1:5678/webhook/uptime-kuma-alert (reaches n8n on port 5678)
docker_cross_network_communication.note: Only works if the target container's port is mapped to the host (e.g. -p 5678:5678)

power_automate_teams_trigger_body_empty.problem: Power Automate 'When a Teams webhook request is received' trigger consumes the HTTP body. triggerBody() returns empty {"":""} for ALL payload formats.
power_automate_teams_trigger_body_empty.consequence: Any subsequent action (Post card, Compose, etc.) cannot access the original request data
power_automate_teams_trigger_body_empty.workaround: Use 'When an HTTP request is received' trigger instead (premium connector) - this preserves triggerBody()

code_node_no_fetch.problem: n8n Code node v2 sandbox does NOT expose global fetch() even though Node.js 22 has it natively
code_node_no_fetch.fix: Use a separate HTTP Request node after the Code node instead of making HTTP calls inside the Code node
code_node_no_fetch.note: The sandbox blocks most Node.js globals. Use Code node only for data transformation, not HTTP calls.

http_request_json_serialization.problem: HTTP Request node with contentType 'json' and dynamic expression in jsonBody causes [object Object] serialization. Expression evaluation + JSON parsing pipeline double-processes the data.
http_request_json_serialization.fix: Use contentType 'raw' with rawContentType 'application/json' and explicit Content-Type header. Code node outputs JSON.stringify(payload), HTTP Request sends it as raw string.

workflow_versioning_system.problem: n8n has a workflow versioning system. Simply updating workflow_entity.nodes does NOT update the running workflow.

architecture.workflow_entity.nodes: Draft version (what you see in the editor)

workflow_versioning_system.consequence: Updating nodes via SQL but not versions causes 'Active version not found' errors or old code executing
workflow_versioning_system.fix: Use n8n CLI (update:workflow/publish:workflow) to properly publish, then restart n8n. Do NOT manually manipulate version tables.

deployment.description: Workflow deployment procedures for DMI n8n instance

update_existing_workflow.description: PREFERRED method for updating workflows already on n8n. Uses delete+reimport because of versioning system.
steps: 2. SCP to VM: scp -i ~/.ssh/internal-tools.pem workflow.json azureuser@172.161.92.118:/tmp/
3. Copy into container: sudo docker cp /tmp/workflow.json n8n:/tmp/
4. Delete old workflow from DB (webhook_entity + workflow_published_version + workflow_entity)
5. Import new: sudo docker exec n8n n8n import:workflow --input=/tmp/workflow.json
6. Activate: sudo docker exec n8n n8n update:workflow --id=NEW_ID --active=true
7. Restart: sudo docker restart n8n && sleep 8
8. Verify webhooks registered in webhook_entity table
9. Clean up temp files on host and in container
update_existing_workflow.warning: Do NOT use raw SQL UPDATE on workflow_entity.nodes — this only updates the draft, not the published version. n8n's versioning system (activeVersionId, workflow_published_version) must be properly updated via CLI.

first_time_import.description: Only for NEW workflows that don't exist yet
first_time_import.command: sudo docker exec n8n n8n import:workflow --input=/tmp/workflow.json
first_time_import.warning: Creates a new workflow each time. NEVER use for updates!

query_workflows.one_liner: sudo docker exec n8n node -e "const s=require('/usr/local/lib/node_modules/n8n/node_modules/.pnpm/sqlite3@5.1.7/node_modules/sqlite3');const d=new s.Database('/home/node/.n8n/database.sqlite');d.all('SELECT id,name,active FROM workflow_entity',(e,r)=>{r.forEach(x=>console.log(JSON.stringify(x)));d.close()});"

teams_integration.description: Microsoft Teams notifications via Power Automate - hard-won production lessons
teams_integration.status: WORKING - Uptime Kuma → n8n → Power Automate (c6ebeea flow) → Teams

power_automate_trigger_behavior.trigger_type: When a Teams webhook request is received
power_automate_trigger_behavior.key_finding: This trigger CONSUMES the request body. triggerBody() always returns {"":""} (empty object) regardless of payload format.
consequences: Any action using triggerBody() (Post card, Compose, etc.) receives EMPTY data
Post card in a chat or channel action ALWAYS fails with 'Property type must be AdaptiveCard' because it parses empty body
This happens for ALL payload formats: MessageCard, Adaptive Card, plain JSON - body is always consumed

auto_posting_behavior.description: The trigger auto-posts MessageCards to the configured channel WITHOUT any action needed
auto_posting_behavior.does_NOT_work_with: Node.js fetch(), axios (n8n HTTP Request node), programmatic HTTP clients
auto_posting_behavior.theory: Difference likely in HTTP headers (Transfer-Encoding, Content-Type charset, User-Agent) between curl and Node.js clients

tested_and_failed_approaches: MessageCard via fetch() in Code node - trigger does not auto-post, Post card action fails
MessageCard via n8n HTTP Request node (axios) - same result
Adaptive Card via HTTP Request node - triggerBody() still empty, Post card action fails
Removing Post card action + Terminate - flow runs but nothing posts
Compose action to inspect triggerBody() - confirmed body is empty {"":""}

option_a.name: Replace Post card with Compose action
option_a.description: Remove 'Post card' action, add 'Compose' with value 'done'. Trigger auto-posts MessageCard. Requires testing if auto-posting works for n8n.

option_b.name: Change trigger to 'When an HTTP request is received'
option_b.description: Use generic HTTP trigger (Request connector) which preserves triggerBody(). Then 'Post card' action can parse Adaptive Card from body. Requires premium connector.

option_c.name: Use Microsoft Graph API directly from n8n
option_c.description: Skip Power Automate entirely. Use n8n Microsoft Teams node with OAuth2 credentials to post directly to channel.

messagecard_format.description: Legacy Office 365 Connector Card format - used by 'When a Teams webhook request is received' trigger

adaptivecard_format.description: Modern card format - required by 'Post card in a chat or channel' action

teams_integration.note: Webhook URL stored in workflow JSON, not here (security)

xml_rpc_integration.description: DokuWiki XML-RPC integration patterns for n8n

common_methods.putPage: wiki.putPage - params: page ID, content, {sum: edit summary}

xml_rpc_integration.response_handling: XML responses stored in $json.data field (auto-detected as text)
xml_rpc_integration.gotcha: Content must be XML-encoded before embedding in XML-RPC body (use encodeXml helper in Code node)

uptime_kuma_teams_relay.name: Uptime Kuma → Teams Relay
uptime_kuma_teams_relay.architecture: Webhook → Build Card (Code, outputs JSON string) → Send to Teams (HTTP Request, raw body) → Respond OK
uptime_kuma_teams_relay.key_pattern: Code node builds payload as JSON.stringify(), HTTP Request sends as contentType 'raw' with rawContentType 'application/json'

uptime_kuma_notification.note: Uses Docker bridge gateway IP because Uptime Kuma and n8n are on different Docker networks

patterns.triggers: Weekly Schedule, Manual Trigger, Webhook Trigger
patterns.http: Get Recent Changes, Save Updated Page
patterns.code: Filter and Categorize Changes, Update Newsticker Content

on_startup.message: n8n context loaded (v2.8 gotchas active). Found {recent_n8n.count} n8n-related memories.
